content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Attached please find the business update for SCS, Lovito and warehouse for
our discussion tomorrow. Thank you!

Best,
Jinyu

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 5

   - Does this take into account increase in comms rate across various
   markets?
- Heard there is some VAT issue, how will that change the picture?
- Will we be able to maintain this profitability April onwards?

- For SG&A budget, we should probably adjust future months budget so
   that it is more accurate?
- When will we have the view that includes SLS cost?

Where did we land
   on how much discount SLS should give us as a big seller?

If we benchmark
   against what they charge other big sellers?

Slide 7

   - Do not understand the BR part
   - ADO >5 includes all markets right?

What is stopping us from reducing
   the ADO requirements to get a wider assortment?

Will we be able to get
   higher ASP items?
- Can explain the bundle situation a bit better?

We can probably play
   with variations a bit better?

For eg. 1 item is $1 but 3 items is $2.50 but
   it is all in 1 listing?

Slide 9

   - How are we measuring backlog rate % right now?
- For pre-sales, how do we measure the conversion rate?

- For the productivity metrics, how do we compare with local scs/SIP
   right now?
- For Taiying, which markets do they serve?
- For BSV, which markets do they serve?
- We wanted to split pre-sales and post-sales?

Is our system able to
   differentiate this right now?

I am not quite sure..



Slide 10

   - So who exactly are we using from march onwards?

The thinking feels
   very haphazard, the backlog so bad, not sure we manage this very well right
   now..



Slide 14

   - For each of this category, how are we thinking about suppliers that we
   source from?

How are we thinking about our allowance in terms # of  stock
   that we bring in as well as blackstock allowance?
- For #3 – How exactly will we identify these SKUs?
- For #4-  How exactly will we identify these SKUs?

Slide 16

   - Can we have ADO numbers as well for this table?

- I need to be able to reconcile the Not in SPU with the #1- #4 on Slide
   14
   - How about Not in CSPU but with Similar Match, where do I find them?
- If we look at contribution of ADO of not in CSPU it is non-existent?
Why is that the case?
- What is the blackstock % of the models Not in CSPU?
- What is the difference in UE of models in CSPU and Not in CSPU?

- In general, we need to probably go much deeper on why we want to grow
   this
      - Is it increasing in orders (right now I do not see it)
      - Is it better margins (right now I do not see it)
      - How this will impact our BS (right now it feels like it is going to
      be very high because the ADO is very low). But maybe it is not
because the
      qty for each model not in CSPU may be low?

Slide 18

   - So should we increase our LFF ADO %?

- For VN and TH, think it is important for us to differentiate between
   NN and Local WH



Slide 19

   - This is very odd, how does the CR numbers compare with SLS sellers who
   use FBS?
They will have the same problems that you are talking about?
- If this was indeed the case, they probably will not want to use FBS?

Slide 28

   - Where did we land on BS consideration?

Slide 29

   - How come SG&A fluctuate so much from Jan >> Feb >>  March?

Slide 31

   - Can we also break it down into listings of various ASPs?

- How do I tell if the ADO % (based on category and ASP) is going
   according to plan?

Slide 32

   - How does the ADO % mix compare with listing % mix right now?

How do we
   tell if it is the right mix that we should be aiming for?

Slide 33

   - 43% is still lower than the Malay buyer base on Shopee
   - Can check with Saovanee on this?

I think it is probably ok as long as
   we are not going into Muslim wear?
And we are not stealing the designs?

- But things like bags/shoes we can definitely push more to them



Slide 35

   - What is the language right now that we are using for MY LS?
- What is the language that we are using for SG LS?

Will we be testing
   for English?

Slide 39

   - So we cannot get in-app assets from FF?

- What is the ADO expectation that we are anticipating from this
   collaboration?

Slide 45

   - Our inbound productivity seems quite low?

And lead time to inbound
   seems long as well?
- How do we define % idle time here?
Feels quite high?
- Can remind me if our WH Ops is 24/7 right now?

Slide 47

   - Not sure I understand this table very well. What is the difference
   between Base Scenario and FBS Scenario?
- Can remind me what our previous projections was?

Slide 52

   - On rate card, where did we land on this in the end?
- How many sellers were we thinking off and the ADO previously?

Is 7
   good/bad?

Slide 54

   - We still seem to be higher than sellers for both GD and GX?

Slide 55

   - Where do I find this 0.125 in Slide 47?
- Even for GD, the sellers do not pay rent?
That cannot be right?

*From:* Soo Siang, Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>
*Sent:* Thursday, 6 March 2025 1:36 pm

content.question: Where did we land on #5?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

I am asking team to build a holistic performance tracking, including volume
and quality of chat bot, human agents, and split by pre-sale and
after-sales etc, and to compare with SIP and local SCS to identify our gaps
and come up with improvement plans.

The tracking will have more data than any other CS trackers that you have
seen, but it take a bit longer time than expected, and we aim to update you
by this week, and to have more discussion on our next week biweekly catchup.

Thanks!


*Best regards,*

*LI Ning*
SCS


Soo Siang, Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>于2025年3月19日 周三下午6:29写道：

content.question:
I see.



Can we also look at the rollout plan?

For eg. How the move to PH will play
a part in this as well?

*From:* LI Ning <ning.li@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Wednesday, 19 March 2025 7:09 pm
*To:* Soo Siang, Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>
*Cc:* Ran Yang <russell.yang@shopee.com>; Jinyu Du <jinyu.du@shopee.com>;
Larry Lin <larry.lin@shopee.com>; Chengchen Huo <chengchen.huo@shopee.com>;
Florence Zhai <florence.zhai@shopee.com>; Roger Yao <roger.yao@shopee.com>;

Mengxiao Yu <william.yu@shopee.com>; Ang Li <ang.lee@shopee.com>; alfred su

Hi Ian,



I am asking team to build a holistic performance tracking, including volume
and quality of chat bot, human agents, and split by pre-sale and
after-sales etc, and to compare with SIP and local SCS to identify our gaps
and come up with improvement plans.



The tracking will have more data than any other CS trackers that you have
seen, but it take a bit longer time than expected, and we aim to update you
by this week, and to have more discussion on our next week biweekly catchup.



Thanks!

Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>于2025年3月19日 周三下午7:15写道：

Hi Ian,

After a comprehensive performance benchmark to local SCS & SIP (by
chatbot/agent, presales/aftersales, etc.), here are our preliminary
findings of our strengths/gaps and improvement plan:

In general, our human agent performance (CSAT, ART, Cost per chat, working
hour scheduling, order conversion rate) is better than local SCS and SIP.
However due to recent low productivity of new hires and lack of sufficient
agents, buyer average queuing time is longer than local SCS and SIP, which
we will expedite the new hire training progress and adding new agents in PH
as per communicated in another email thread. On the other hand, our chatbot
deflection rate is much better than local SCS, however we found there is
some gap in conversion between chatbot and human agent performance, which
we will work with BPM to see how to improve chatbot conversion, thus to
further grow the sales.

Moreover, we found comparing to SEA, our BR CS performance isn't as good.
Besides BR using a different chatbot model, we think the time zone,
language and culture differences are also the reasons while hosting BR CS
in Asia. We will study more and update in next step.

More detailed findings as following:

   -

   CB SCS performs better at: 1) Agent CSAT; 2) ART (avg. response time);
   3) Cost per chat; 4) Working hour scheduling; 5) Agent order conversion rate
   -

      Agent CSAT
      -

         Performance: CB SCS (SEA+TW) 80.0%, local SCS 76.9%, SIP 70.3%
         -

         Reason: CB SCS sets agent CSAT as core KPI of external vendor
         (weight 50%), which linked to monthly bonus paid to them
         -

         Next step: We observed the CSAT of new hires (70%~75%, ~22 agents)
         is significantly lower than existing agents (80~85%, ~26
agents). We have
         started daily standup meeting last week with new-hires to accelerate
         improvement, and have asked BSV (our CS vendor) to replace
those new hires
